title: Incorrectly Managed Min Heap Height in Binary Heap

statement.markdown: You are given a list of integers. The task is to return the minimum height of the binary heap that can be formed from the given list of integers. A binary heap is a complete binary tree where for every node i, the value of the node is either greater than (max heap) or less than (min heap) the values of its children.
statement.inputFormat: Input a list of integers as a space-separated list
statement.outputFormat: The minimum height of the binary heap
statement.notes: You should use the min heap data structure to solve the problem.

examples.explanation: The binary heap with height 2 has the minimum height among all possible binary heaps that can be formed from the given list of integers.

hints.text: Use the Fibonacci heap or the binomial heap instead of the binary heap if the time complexity is not a concern.

editorial.title: Editorial
editorial.intuition: The problem requires the use of the binary heap data structure. We will first create a binary heap and then try to get the minimum height of the binary heap.
editorial.bruteForce: Create a binary heap and then try to get the minimum height of the binary heap.
editorial.betterSolution: Use the Fibonacci heap or the binomial heap instead of the binary heap.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use a priority queue to store the nodes of the binary heap and then try to get the minimum height of the binary heap.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: To prove the correctness of the optimal solution, we need to prove that the priority queue is a valid binary heap.
editorial.dryRun: Create a priority queue and then try to get the minimum height of the binary heap.
commonMistakes: Not using a priority queue to store the nodes of the binary heap
Not trying to get the minimum height of the binary heap
